abstract: This paper examines how online incivility is normalized and negotiated on TikTok in the Philippines through a phenomenon known as 'dogshows,' in which Gen Z influencers and their audiences engage in legal-but-harmful humor. Drawing on platform affordances and cultural practices, the authors explore how such humor blurs lines between entertainment, cyberbullying, and hate speech. The study contributes to understandings of how humor and incivility are amplified through influencer cultures on TikTok.
key_claims: Online incivility on TikTok in the Philippines is performed, accepted, and negotiated through a culturally specific phenomenon called 'dogshows'.
Humor on TikTok can function as a vehicle for legal-but-harmful content that approaches cyberbullying and hate speech.
TikTok's affordances actively shape and amplify cultures of incivility among Gen Z influencers and their audiences.
methods: Qualitative study of TikTok influencer practices and audience interactions in the Filipino context
Analysis of TikTok platform affordances in relation to incivility cultures
findings: The 'dogshow' phenomenon exemplifies how incivility becomes an accepted social practice among Filipino Gen Z TikTok users
Influencers and audiences collaboratively maneuver legal-but-harmful humor in ways that normalize incivility
TikTok affordances facilitate the amplification of such humor-based incivility (further details not available from abstract)
contributions: Introduces and analyzes 'dogshows' as a localized form of online incivility on TikTok in the Philippines
Extends the concept of legal-but-harmful humor (Matamoros-Fernández et al., 2023) into the Filipino Gen Z influencer context
Adds to scholarship linking platform affordances, humor, and cultures of online incivility
framing: The paper situates itself within scholarship on digital platforms, online incivility, and humor as a mechanism of cyberbullying and hate speech. It builds on Matamoros-Fernández et al.'s (2023) notion of legal-but-harmful humor and engages with platform studies of TikTok, foregrounding non-Western (Filipino) influencer cultures.
